# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    @products = Product.all

    search_params = params[:search]

    if search_params.present?
      @products = filter_by_name(@products, search_params[:name])
      @products = filter_by_category(@products, search_params[:category])
      @products = filter_by_price_range(@products, search_params[:min_price], search_params[:max_price])
      @products = filter_by_brand(@products, search_params[:brand])
      @products = filter_by_availability(@products, search_params[:in_stock])
      @products = filter_by_rating(@products, search_params[:min_rating])
      @products = apply_sorting(@products, search_params[:sort_by], search_params[:sort_direction])
    end

    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(params[:per_page] || 24)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: { products: @products, meta: pagination_meta(@products) } }
    end
  end

  private

  def filter_by_name(scope, name)
    return scope if name.blank?

    # SAFE: Using parameterized query with sanitize_sql_like to prevent SQL injection
    scope.where("name ILIKE ?", "%#{ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(name)}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(scope, category)
    return scope if category.blank?

    if category.is_a?(Array)
      scope.where(category_id: category.map(&:to_i))
    else
      scope.where(category_id: category.to_i)
    end
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(scope, min_price, max_price)
    if min_price.present? && max_price.present?
      scope.where(price: BigDecimal(min_price.to_s)..BigDecimal(max_price.to_s))
    elsif min_price.present?
      scope.where("price >= ?", BigDecimal(min_price.to_s))
    elsif max_price.present?
      scope.where("price <= ?", BigDecimal(max_price.to_s))
    else
      scope
    end
  rescue ArgumentError
    # If price values can't be converted to BigDecimal, skip this filter
    scope
  end

  def filter_by_brand(scope, brand)
    return scope if brand.blank?

    if brand.is_a?(Array)
      sanitized_brands = brand.select(&:present?)
      return scope if sanitized_brands.empty?

      scope.where(brand: sanitized_brands)
    else
      scope.where(brand: brand)
    end
  end

  def filter_by_availability(scope, in_stock)
    return scope if in_stock.blank?

    if ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(in_stock)
      scope.where("stock_quantity > ?", 0)
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  def filter_by_rating(scope, min_rating)
    return scope if min_rating.blank?

    rating = min_rating.to_f
    return scope unless rating.between?(0, 5)

    scope.where("average_rating >= ?", rating)
  end

  def apply_sorting(scope, sort_by, sort_direction)
    allowed_sort_columns = %w[name price created_at average_rating stock_quantity]
    allowed_directions = %w[asc desc]

    column = allowed_sort_columns.include?(sort_by) ? sort_by : "created_at"
    direction = allowed_directions.include?(sort_direction&.downcase) ? sort_direction.downcase : "desc"

    scope.order(Arel.sql("#{column} #{direction}"))
  end

  def pagination_meta(collection)
    {
      current_page: collection.current_page,
      total_pages: collection.total_pages,
      total_count: collection.total_count,
      per_page: collection.limit_value
    }
  end
end
